🌱 **gardener** · 🆕 `NEW_TERRITORY` · severity: `medium` · commit: `9c3e91cf` > **What is this?** repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's **product decisions, architecture, and roadmap** — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit. ### Context fit
Context match | Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|---------|---------------|-----| | Bulk agent fleet controls | Adds `BulkAgentControls.tsx` (1231 lines) with start/stop/restart/free-memory for all agents in a company, confirmation dialogs, and per-agent operation planning | Governance NODE: Board Powers include agent lifecycle control; Agent Model NODE: agents have lifecycle states (paused, error, active). Bulk fleet operations are not explicitly documented as a product surface. | 🆕 New | | Provider quota monitoring | `summarizeProviderQuota` detects OAuth vs API-key source, shows `Claude Max (OAuth)` labeling, flow-bottleneck detection for blocked/stale issues | Product NODE: "Surface problems, don't hide them" — quota visibility aligns with this principle. Provider health monitoring as a board-level surface is not in the tree. | 🆕 New | | Dashboard sync controls | Sync Dashboard Copy button to pull latest Paperclip code into a dashboard-owned repo copy | No tree guidance on dashboard-repo sync as a product feature. | 🆕 New | | OAuth vs agent-key distinction | When OAuth quota hits 100%, clarifies "agent API key calls unaffected" | Adapter abstraction: agents connect through adapters with their own auth. The distinction between OAuth (human operator) and API key (agent) quota is operationally important but undocumented. | 🆕 New |
Tree nodes referenced - [`product/governance/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — Board Powers, agent lifecycle control. - [`product/agent-model/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/agent-model/NODE.md) — Agent lifecycle states, adapter protocol. - [`product/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — "Surface problems, don't hide them"; Board-level abstraction. - [`engineering/frontend/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/frontend/NODE.md) — React component patterns, page structure. - [`adapters/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/adapters/NODE.md) — Adapter auth separation (agent keys vs OAuth).
### Recommendation **Why:** While the PR title says "fix" for OAuth quota labeling, the diff adds a 1231-line component that introduces several new product surfaces: bulk fleet controls (start/stop/restart all agents), provider quota health monitoring, flow bottleneck detection, and a dashboard-repo sync feature. These are operationally useful and spirit-aligned with "Board-level abstraction" (answering "what is the company doing" and "what needs my approval"), but they represent significant new territory: 1. **Bulk fleet operations** — The tree documents individual agent lifecycle control as a board power, but fleet-wide start/stop/restart with plan-then-execute semantics is a new control pattern that should be documented. 2. **Provider quota monitoring** — The distinction between OAuth (operator) and API-key (agent) quota is operationally important and should be captured under governance or the adapter model. 3. **Dashboard sync** — Syncing a dashboard-owned Paperclip repo copy is deployment infrastructure surfaced as a product feature; the tree has no guidance here. **Suggested path forward:** Document the bulk fleet control pattern and provider quota monitoring under the appropriate tree nodes. The OAuth/API-key distinction is particularly worth capturing since it affects how operators reason about agent availability vs. their own quota limits. --- Reviewed commit: 9c3e91cf · Tree snapshot: ddfe6b6 · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). Reviews this repo against [serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree), a user-maintained context tree. Not affiliated with this project's maintainers.

🌱 **gardener** · 🆕 `NEW_TERRITORY` · severity: `medium` · commit: `419c898` > **What is this?** repo-gardener checks whether PRs and issues fit the project's **product decisions, architecture, and roadmap** — not code correctness. Think of it as a product-context review layer. For code review, see Greptile/CodeRabbit. ### Context fit
Context fit | Area | This PR | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|---------|---------------|-----| | Execution workspaces | Adds a full pull request policy lifecycle (request/result handshake, blocking mode, timeout scheduler) to execution workspace close flow | Execution workspaces exist but PR policy is not yet covered in the context tree | 🆕 New | | Plugin event system | Three new plugin event types for PR lifecycle | Plugin system is first-class; event bus is established infrastructure | ✅ Aligned | | Control plane boundary | Paperclip orchestrates the PR request/result handshake; actual git/provider work is done by external consumers | "Control plane, not execution plane" — Paperclip never runs agent code directly | ✅ Aligned | | Governance/audit | Activity logging for all PR state transitions; row-locked updates prevent race conditions | Governance-first with full audit trails | ✅ Aligned | | Anti-requirements | No git provider dependencies added; credential/runtime footprint unchanged | Paperclip is not a delivery infrastructure manager | ✅ Aligned |
### Recommendation **Why:** This is a substantial, well-designed feature that introduces a typed PR policy contract for execution workspaces. The tree does not currently cover execution workspace PR policies, so this is new territory — but the implementation is consistent with existing architectural patterns (control plane boundary, plugin events, governance audit trails). The PR is opened as a draft, and the author has flagged ROADMAP coordination. **Suggested path forward:** This aligns well with the project's principles and would benefit from maintainer review of the design scope, particularly how it relates to the ROADMAP's "Enforced Outcomes" milestone. If accepted, the context tree should be updated to document the PR policy contract in `engineering/backend/` or a new execution workspace sub-domain.
Tree nodes referenced - [`product/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — Control plane, not execution plane; anti-requirements - [`plugins/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/plugins/NODE.md) — Plugin event system and capability-gated access - [`engineering/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/engineering/NODE.md) — Service-oriented backend, monorepo structure - [`product/governance/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — Audit trails and governance actions
--- Reviewed commit: 419c898 · Tree snapshot: ddfe6b6 · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). | Area | This issue | Tree guidance | Fit | |------|-----------|---------------|-----| | Adapter architecture | Gateway adapter can't inject per-run JWT because the OpenClaw protocol has no `env` field and the agent is already running | Tree confirms gateway adapters connect via WebSocket to an already-running agent, unlike local adapters that spawn child processes with injected env vars | ✅ Aligned | | Security: JWT on disk | Proposed fix writes run-specific JWT to `~/.openclaw/workspace-{agentId}/paperclip-claimed-api-key.json` | Tree notes governance-first design: "every mutation is logged with actor, action, and before/after state." Writing JWTs to disk introduces a credential persistence surface not covered by existing guidance | ❓ Partial | | Adapter contract | Proposes a side-channel (file system) for credential passing outside the `ServerAdapterModule` interface | Tree says adapters compose utilities from adapter-utils (`buildPaperclipEnv`, `runChildProcess`). A file-based side channel is a new pattern not in the current contract | 🆕 New |
### Recommendation **Why:** The bug report is well-documented and clearly real — gateway adapters fundamentally cannot inject per-run environment variables the way local adapters do. However, the proposed fix introduces two concerns that need architectural review: (1) writing JWTs to disk creates a credential persistence surface that the current adapter contract doesn't account for, and (2) a file-based side channel is a new inter-process communication pattern outside the established `ServerAdapterModule` interface. **Suggested path forward:** Consider whether the OpenClaw gateway protocol itself should be extended with an `env` or `context` field (upstream fix), or whether adapter-utils should formalize a secure side-channel mechanism that other gateway adapters could also use. If the file approach is chosen, it should include cleanup on run completion and appropriate file permissions.

### Recommendation **Why:** The tree documents explicit delegation as the task assignment model ("Delegation = creating a task and assigning it"). Proactive self-assignment introduces a new pattern where agents autonomously claim work from backlog/todo queues. While the atomic checkout mechanism supports safe claiming, this changes the coordination model — an agent scanning and grabbing work is fundamentally different from being assigned work by a manager or human. The opt-in toggle (default: off) mitigates risk, but the pattern itself is undocumented. **Suggested path forward:** This is a reasonable feature that the opt-in default handles well. Consider adding a brief section under `product/agent-model/NODE.md` documenting proactive self-assignment as a new capability pattern — when it fires, what scope it considers, and how it interacts with the delegation model. This helps future contributors understand the two task acquisition paths (explicit delegation vs. proactive claiming). --- Reviewed commit: eb3647e · Tree snapshot: fff6709 · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). ### Summary This PR fixes two real bugs that prevent the Slack plugin from working correctly: 1. **Slack HMAC verification** — `express.json()` was the only body parser, so form-encoded Slack slash commands never had `rawBody` populated. HMAC verification always failed. The fix adds `express.urlencoded()` with a `verify` callback alongside the JSON parser. 2. **Plugin event duplication** — When a plugin registers multiple `ctx.events.on()` subscriptions for the same event pattern (e.g., two `issue.created` handlers), the event bus delivered N copies of the same event to the worker. The fix deduplicates via an `eventId`-based Map with a 10-second sliding window, collapsing N deliveries into 1. Both changes are **pragmatic and localized**: - They don't affect plugin process isolation, capability enforcement, or data persistence (which the tree marks as core). - The in-memory dedup cache is acceptable for transient collision prevention. - The documented gap (eventId-less events can still duplicate) is acknowledged and not a blocker. ✅ **Fit:** This PR aligns with the plugin architecture as described in the tree. It fixes bugs at the boundary between the backend webhook handler and the plugin event bus without violating the architectural constraints (process isolation, capability gating, plugin-worker contract). --- Reviewed commit: 85d8a3a · Tree snapshot: 6e68dbe · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). **Summary:** Fixes Codex adapter to preserve managed auth (API key vs ChatGPT session) and repo-root `.env` loading. When `OPENAI_API_KEY` is present, the managed `CODEX_HOME` prefers API-key auth instead of inheriting a shared login session. Also fixes Gemini adapter stdout line parsing.

**Tree context:**
- `adapters/codex-local/NODE.md` — Codex CLI adapter. Managed `CODEX_HOME` and environment probes are core adapter responsibilities.
- `adapters/NODE.md` — Adapters handle lifecycle, workspace management, and billing inference. Environment configuration is adapter-side.

**Observations (re-review):**
1. 11 files changed (448 additions, 19 deletions) — mostly new tests covering auth mode selection and env loading.
2. Includes Gemini adapter fix for parsing stdout lines — minor cross-adapter improvement.
3. Updates `server/src/config.ts` for repo-root `.env` loading order.
4. Previous review was ALIGNED; new commits expand test coverage. Verdict remains ALIGNED.

**Verdict:** Adapter environment configuration fix with comprehensive test coverage. Follows established adapter patterns.

Direct fix for issue #3264 by the same reporter (`@harperaa`) who filed it — nice closing of the loop. The change matches the proposed fix exactly: instead of enumerating board routes and stripping only those, the new logic strips the company prefix whenever the first segment is not a global or board route. That's the right inversion — it treats plugin page routes as first-class citizens of the routing layer without requiring every new plugin to register itself in a central enumeration, which aligns with the tree's "thin core, rich edges" stance toward plugins.

The regression tests are well-structured: three separate test cases covering plugin page routes (the bug), board routes (to prove the fix doesn't regress existing behavior), and global routes (to prove the fix doesn't over-strip). The docstring explicitly links back to issue #3264, which makes the test self-documenting for future readers.

Two-line code change, 24-line test file. Perfect scope. Once this lands, issue #3264 can be closed.

A single fix to the JSONL parser in `packages/adapters/opencode-local/src/server/parse.ts` (or equivalent) should address both. Worth linking the two issues and treating them as one work item. ### Recommendation — solution ranking The reporter proposes three solutions. Ranking them by tree alignment and blast radius: 1. **Fix the JSONL parser first** (implied by #3193 but not yet done). This is the root-cause fix: tool errors stay in the transcript, only fatal adapter errors bubble up as run-level failures. Scoped to the adapter package, no cross-domain changes. Do this regardless of the other options. 2. **OpenCode server API transition** (reporter's option 1) is architecturally attractive but much larger scope — it would enable persistent sessions and finer-grained error recovery, but deserves a dedicated design discussion and tree node under `adapters/opencode-local/` describing the lifecycle shift. Not a prerequisite for fixing this bug. 3. **Retry-loop logic in Paperclip** (reporter's option 2) should be avoided — it crosses the adapter boundary. Paperclip's control plane shouldn't know about "adapter_failed" strings or special-case opencode error patterns; that's the adapter's job to classify correctly. 4. **`--session`/`--continue` flags** (reporter's option 3) is a useful supplement to option 1, but only after the parser is fixed. Clean adapter feature. Tree alignment is straightforward:

- **Surface problems, don't hide them**: the new `codex_auth_required` error code is a structured signal, not a silent failure
- **Unified API**: the agent-detail UI action is a parallel of the existing Claude login button — one UI surface, adapter-specific wiring behind it

One small note: the PR description mentions `pnpm build` still fails on Windows because the repo build script uses `cp -r`. That's unrelated to this PR and worth a separate issue so it doesn't block Windows contributors.

🌱 **gardener:verdict:** `ALIGNED` · severity: `low` · commit: `fdecac8e`

Aligned with context tree. No concerns that block merge, but a few worth noting for reviewers.

The core change is a clean single-source-of-truth refactor: three UI codepaths (`NewAgent`, `OnboardingWizard`, `AgentConfigForm`) each carried their own inline per-adapter default dictionaries, which had drifted. Extracting `adapterConfigDefaults` + `getCreateValuesForAdapterType()` eliminates the drift. Server-side normalization in `applyAdapterConfigDefaults` is actually MORE correct than UI-side defaults alone — API callers (CLI, scripts, plugins) now get the default too, not just users creating via the web UI. Good instinct.

### Notes for reviewers

1. **PR scope is broader than the description suggests.** The PR body says "UI-only default logic" and "no server, or adapter runtime changes" but the diff includes:
    - Server-side normalization in `server/src/routes/agents.ts` (`applyAdapterConfigDefaults` now writes a `dangerouslySkipPermissions` default for `claude_local` and `opencode_local`)
    - A behavior change to `asBoolean` in `packages/adapter-utils/src/server-utils.ts` (now accepts string `"true"`/`"false"` case-insensitively, was boolean-only)
    - A new 314-line `dangerously-skip-permissions.test.ts`

    Both extra changes are safe and well-tested, but the "low risk, UI-only" framing is misleading. Worth updating the PR description so CODEOWNERS/reviewers see the full scope.

2. **Interaction with PR #3294 (still open).** PR #3294 introduces `resolveDangerouslySkipPermissions()` in the claude-local adapter that overrides the configured value to `false` when Paperclip runs as root. If both PRs land, the data flow becomes:
    - UI writes `dangerouslySkipPermissions: true` from the shared default
    - Server normalizes to `true` if missing (this PR)
    - Adapter overrides to `false` at execute time on root (#3294)

    The config stored in the DB says one thing; the runtime does another. That's not a bug per se, but it creates a surprising diagnostic experience — operators will see `dangerouslySkipPermissions: true` in agent config but no `--dangerously-skip-permissions` flag in the transcript. Worth coordinating with #3294 or adding a logger.info explaining the override when it fires.

3. **`asBoolean` widening is a cross-cutting behavior change.** The new string coercion (`"true"` → `true`, `"false"` → `false`, case-insensitive) affects every caller of `asBoolean` across the codebase. The widening is backwards-compatible (existing boolean inputs behave identically), and the coercion only fires on string inputs that previously fell through to the fallback. The test coverage is thorough. Low risk, but worth calling out because adapter-utils contract changes are the kind of thing that can surprise plugin authors or downstream adapters — the utility's signature lives in `@paperclipai/adapter-utils` which is part of the stable surface.

4. **`dangerouslySkipPermissions: true` as the default** is a load-bearing security decision. The inline comment explains it correctly (headless mode has no interactive prompts), but it's worth documenting in the context tree under `adapters/` as an explicit policy so future adapter authors know the rationale and don't re-litigate it.

### Recommendation

Land it. The refactor is good, the server-side enforcement is the right layer, and the tests are solid. Just (a) update the PR description to reflect the full scope, (b) add a coordination note to PR #3294 about the config-vs-runtime override interaction, and (c) consider adding a one-paragraph note to `adapters/NODE.md` or the tree about the "headless mode default skip" policy so the rationale is discoverable.

🌱 **gardener:verdict:** `ALIGNED` · severity: `low` · commit: `b853ad7a`

Aligned with context tree. No concerns.

Excellent fix. The author correctly identified that `listAdapterModels(type)` and `detectAdapterModel(type)` ignore `companyId` entirely — the `assertCompanyAccess` gate was guarding nothing and silently breaking the onboarding dropdown. Replacing it with `assertBoard` matches the tree's "Unified API, Different Authorization" pattern: the auth check reflects what data is actually exposed, not what the URL looks like.

What makes this PR stand out is the careful scoping discipline: `POST /test-environment` is explicitly left strict because it resolves per-company secrets via `secretService.normalizeAdapterConfigForPersistence` / `resolveAdapterConfigForRuntime` — loosening that one would be a real cross-company leak. Drawing that exact line, and explaining it in both the PR description and an inline test comment, is the right level of care for an auth change.

Test coverage is thoughtful too — the 95-line test file explicitly notes the `local_implicit` god-mode bypass and constructs realistic remote session-board actors to exercise the cross-company path. This closes a subtle test gap where default `createApp()` would have hidden the fix.

Also worth noting: this is yet another instance of the "silent failure" pattern I've been flagging in recent items (#3282, #3215, #3207, #3284). The 403 → empty dropdown with no clear error is the exact anti-pattern the tree's "surface problems, don't hide them" principle calls out. Worth a general audit: any endpoint gated by `assertCompanyAccess` that doesn't actually read `companyId` in its handler is likely misscoped for the same reason.

🌱 **gardener:verdict:** `ALIGNED` · severity: `low` · commit: `7f7157a3`

Aligned with context tree. No concerns.

Clean 9-line bug fix. Three wakeup payloads in `routes/issues.ts` were passing `req.params.id` (the human-readable identifier like `AIU-67`) instead of the already-resolved `issue.id` UUID, silently breaking @-mention wakeups and comment-triggered wakeups — agents never woke up for mentions. Adjacent code for assignment-wake already used the correct pattern, so this fix brings all wakeup sites into consistency.

This bug is the 4th instance of the same root pattern I've flagged in recent items (#3282 silent filter, #3215 orphaned processes, #3207 silent cleanup): the control plane fails silently rather than surfacing the problem, which the tree explicitly calls out as an anti-pattern. The fix is correct in its current scope, but consider two follow-ups as separate tasks:

1. Add a loud error log or activity event when the heartbeat service fails to resolve an `issueId` — a silent miss should be observable.
2. A route-level integration test for @-mention wakeups would prevent regression. The PR author notes this would require a full HTTP test harness; worth the investment given how load-bearing inter-agent communication is.

🌱 **gardener:verdict:** `ALIGNED` · severity: `low` · commit: `6a3ca993`

Upgraded from `NEEDS_REVIEW · medium`. Aligned with context tree. No blocking concerns.

The new commit (`6a3ca993` — "fix(goals): audit every verification state transition") is a thorough and well-crafted response to the primary blocker from the prior review. Every verification state transition now writes an `activity_log` entry:

- `goal.verification_requested` — when a new cycle starts; captures attempt number, manual vs auto, criteria count
- `goal.achieved` — the critical governance-level transition, explicitly tagged `via: "verification_outcome"` so it's distinguishable from other achievement paths
- `goal.verification_incomplete` — missing criteria with their IDs
- `goal.verification_unclear` — audit even for "don't count as failure" cases, which is exactly right (the tree's "surface problems" principle covers ambiguous outcomes, not just failures)
- `goal.verification_failed` — failing criterion IDs and the follow-up issue ID

The `VerificationActor` type is well-designed. System stand-in for the auto-fire hook path, real actor threaded through for manual/issue-completion paths, with a clear comment explaining the semantics: "the audit actor for verification-driven goal transitions is the agent (or user) who PATCHed the triggering issue to done... semantically right for both branches." That's exactly the tree's "every mutation is logged with actor, action, and outcome" principle applied cleanly.

The inline comment on the `goal.achieved` entry deserves a specific callout: *"The ACHIEVED transition is the critical auditable event here. It moves a goal from pending/active to achieved without direct human approval (per the tree's governance semantics) — the audit row captures which verification issue drove it and who was acting."* That's the right framing — the PR author is treating auto-achievement as something the Board can retroactively review rather than pre-approve, and making sure the audit trail supports that.

### Residual notes (not blocking)

Two of the three questions from the prior review are still open, but neither blocks merge:

1. **Is auto goal `achieved` a V1 governance gate?** — This is a tree-docs question, not a code question. The PR now makes the transition auditable, so if the Board decides later that auto-achievement should require approval, that's a straightforward follow-up. Worth recording the decision in `product/governance/NODE.md` as either "auto-achievement is in-scope for V1, audit covers it" or "auto-achievement requires a new approval gate" — either answer is fine.

2. **Document the `verification_outcome` fenced infostring in the tree.** The custom `json verification_outcome` fence is a load-bearing agent contract that future adapters and prompt authors need to know about. Worth a short note under `product/task-system/` or `adapters/` describing the format so it's discoverable without reading the goal-verification source.

Neither should block this PR.

Tree nodes referenced - [`product/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/NODE.md) — "Thin core, rich edges": external integrations go into plugins; governance-first - [`product/governance/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/product/governance/NODE.md) — Board hard-stops and budget threshold events already defined; board oversight is non-negotiable - [`plugins/NODE.md`](https://github.com/serenakeyitan/paperclip-tree/blob/main/plugins/NODE.md) — Plugin event subscriptions and outbound HTTP via `ctx.http.fetch`; plugin extensibility is the designed escape hatch
### Recommendation The maintainers need to decide: is notification delivery infrastructure (email/webhook routing) a governance primitive that belongs in core, or an integration concern that belongs in a first-party plugin? Both positions are defensible: - **Core argument:** Board reachability is a safety property, not a feature. If agents can't reliably page the human, governance fails silently — which directly contradicts "surface problems, don't hide them." - **Plugin argument:** Email providers (Resend, SES, SMTP), webhook formats, and filtering logic vary per operator. The "thin core, rich edges" principle exists precisely to avoid baking in opinionated integrations that not every operator wants. A middle path worth considering: add a lightweight **notification hook abstraction** in core (event → configurable webhook POST) with no email logic, and ship first-party email plugins separately. This preserves the thin core while making the default experience work out of the box. The reporter's `board-notify` plugin demonstrates the plugin path is viable today. If the plugin SDK has the right event hooks and capability grants, promoting a reference plugin to "bundled by default" may be the right answer rather than adding email logic to the server. --- Reviewed issue updated: 2026-04-10T02:08:04Z · Tree snapshot: 33dc6fd · Commands: @gardener re-review · @gardener pause · @gardener ignore 🌱 Posted by [repo-gardener](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/repo-gardener) — an open-source context-aware review bot built on [First-Tree](https://github.com/agent-team-foundation/first-tree). > **gardener:verdict:** `NEEDS_REVIEW` · severity: `medium` · commit: `ccd7776`

---

## Summary

This PR is titled as a bug fix for plugin page route prefix-doubling, and that fix is correct and aligned. However, the PR bundles four substantial and largely unrelated changes under a misleading title: a new BastionClaw adapter (filesystem IPC + SQLite polling), a company-level kill switch (pause/resume), a heartbeat model override feature, and the actual bug fix. The BastionClaw adapter introduces a new architectural pattern not recorded in the context tree, and there is a SQL injection risk in the adapter's polling loop.

---

## Context Match

| Area | Tree | PR | Match |
|---|---|---|---|
| Plugin page route fix (`company-routes.ts`) | Plugin extensibility is core; routing correctness required | Correctly strips company prefix for any non-global segment | ✅ Aligned |
| Company pause/resume (kill switch) | Governance: "Board can pause/resume any Agent immediately"; company lifecycle has `paused` status | Board-only `POST /:companyId/pause` + `POST /:companyId/resume`, cancels active runs | ✅ Aligned |
| Heartbeat skips paused companies | Company lifecycle: "Paused companies stop all heartbeats" | `tickTimers` now queries paused companies and skips their agents | ✅ Aligned |
| BastionClaw adapter pattern | Tree lists gateway adapters as WebSocket-based (`openclaw-gateway`); "gateway adapters connect to a remote agent over WebSocket" | BastionClaw uses **filesystem IPC + SQLite polling** — not WebSocket | ⚠️ New Territory |
| Heartbeat model override | No tree node records adapter-level model override at heartbeat invocation | `heartbeat.model` added to heartbeat policy, injected into `runtimeConfig` on timer runs only | ⚠️ New Territory |

---

## Issues

### 1. SQL injection risk in BastionClaw adapter (medium severity)

In `packages/adapters/bastionclaw/src/server/execute.ts`, the task poll query interpolates `taskId` directly into an SQL string with only single-quote escaping:

```ts
`SELECT status, result, error, duration_ms FROM task_run_logs WHERE task_id = '${taskId.replace(/'/g, "''")}' ORDER BY run_at DESC LIMIT 1`
```

`taskId` is constructed as `paperclip-${ctx.runId}` where `ctx.runId` comes from the server. While the server controls `runId` today, this pattern bypasses parameterized queries and creates a brittle trust assumption. The `sqlite3` CLI used here supports parameterized queries via `-cmd`. This should use a parameterized form or at minimum assert that `taskId` contains only safe characters before interpolation.

### 2. PR bundles four unrelated changes under a misleading title (low-medium)

The title "Fix plugin page routes doubling company prefix on company switch" covers only ~10 lines of the 3,800-line diff. The rest introduces: a new adapter, a company kill switch feature, a heartbeat model override, GitHub issue templates, and a SECURITY.md. Bundling makes review harder and commit history less useful. These should be separate PRs.

### 3. BastionClaw adapter pattern is not in the tree (new territory)

The adapters tree node defines two patterns: **local** (spawns a child process) and **gateway** (WebSocket). BastionClaw uses a third pattern: **filesystem IPC + SQLite polling**. The adapter writes a JSON task file to a shared directory and polls a SQLite DB for completion. This is a novel integration pattern with meaningful tradeoffs (local-only, polling latency, SQLite lock sensitivity) that should be captured in the tree before the adapter ships, so future adapters don't re-litigate this design.

### 4. `models: []` in BastionClaw index.ts

`packages/adapters/bastionclaw/src/index.ts` exports `models: []`. The tree notes that adapters report model info via `detectModel`. If BastionClaw can detect the model from the Claude instance it dispatches to, this should be wired up rather than left empty.

---

## Tree Nodes Referenced

- `adapters/NODE.md` — local vs gateway pattern, `ServerAdapterModule` interface
- `adapters/openclaw-gateway/NODE.md` — gateway adapter precedent
- `product/governance/NODE.md` — board powers, pause/resume, budget hard-stops
- `product/company-model/NODE.md` — company lifecycle (`active`/`paused`/`archived`)
- `product/NODE.md` — control plane principles

---

## Recommendation

The routing fix and the governance/heartbeat changes are aligned and look correct. The **BastionClaw adapter** is new territory worth flagging:

1. **Fix the SQL injection** before merge — use parameterized queries or character-validation on `taskId`.
2. **Confirm the IPC+SQLite pattern is intentional** and draft a `adapters/bastionclaw/NODE.md` tree node capturing the design rationale and constraints (local-only, SQLite polling, requires BastionClaw to be co-located).
